---
title: "Exponential Bradley-Terry: bias for MLE with three runners"
author: "R. K. S. Hankin"
output: bookdown::html_document2
bibliography: hyper2.bib
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
set.seed(1)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library("hyper2")
library("knitr")
```

```{r out.width='20%', out.extra='style="float:right; padding:10px"',echo=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics(system.file("help/figures/hyper2.png", package = "hyper2"))
```

To cite the `hyper2` package in publications, please use @hankin2017_rmd.

Document `hankin-exponential_BT.tex` refers.

```{r, label=showthree, echo=FALSE, out.width = "100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("123all.png")  # file 123all.png created from
                                       # 123all.pdf, which is produced
                                       # by exponential_BT.Rmd
```

So what we are going to do is to find out the sampling distribution
for the maximum likelihood estimator, conditional on
$r_\mbox{true}=1,2,3$.  Recall that the BT strengths for the three
competitors are $x^1,x^2,x^3$, and we use standard Plackett-Luce
likelihoods for the order statistics.


We need to find where the $r=1$ and $r=2$ lines cross at
$\sqrt{(\sqrt{5}-1)/2}\simeq 0.786$.


# First case:  $x<\sqrt{(\sqrt{5}-1)/2}$

Here the lines behave themselves and the MLEs are easy:

$r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow \hat{r}=1$

$r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow \hat{r}=2$

$r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow \hat{r}=3$


## First: $r_\mbox{true}=1$ {-}

\begin{eqnarray}
P(r_\mbox{obs}=1)
&=& P({\mathbf 1}\succ 2\succ 3) + P({\mathbf 1}\succ 3\succ 2)\\
&=&\frac{x^1}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\\
&=&\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\\
&{}&\\
P(r_\mbox{obs}=2) &=& P(2\succ {\mathbf 1}\succ 3) + P(3\succ {\mathbf
1}\succ 2)\\
&=& \frac{x^2}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^1}{x^1+x^3} +
    \frac{x^3}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^1}{x^2+x^3}\\
&=& \frac{1}{1+x}+\frac{1}{1+x^2}-\frac{2}{1+x+x^2}\\
&{}&\\
P(r_\mbox{obs}=3) &=& P(2\succ 3\succ {\mathbf 1}) + P(3\succ 2\succ
{\mathbf 1})\\
&=& \frac{x^2}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^3}{x^1+x^3} +
    \frac{x^3}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^2}{x^1+x^3}\\ 
&=& 1-\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{1+x^2}+\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}
\end{eqnarray}

So we can see that $P(r_\mbox{obs}=1) + P(r_\mbox{obs}=2) +
P(r_\mbox{obs}=3)=1$.


## Second: $r_\mbox{true}=2$ {-}

\begin{eqnarray}
P(r_\mbox{obs}=1)
&=& P({\mathbf 2}\succ 1\succ 3) + P({\mathbf 2}\succ 3\succ 1)\\
&=&\frac{x^2}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\\
&=&\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}\\
&{}&\\
P(r_\mbox{obs}=2) &=& P(1\succ {\mathbf 2}\succ 3) + P(3\succ {\mathbf
2}\succ 1)\\
&=& \frac{x^1}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^2}{x^2+x^3} +
    \frac{x^3}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^2}{x^1+x^2}\\
&=& 1-\frac{2x}{1+x+x^2}\\
&{}&\\
P(r_\mbox{obs}=3) &=& P(1\succ 3\succ {\mathbf 2}) + P(3\succ 1\succ
{\mathbf 2})\\
&=& \frac{x^1}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^3}{x^2+x^3} +
    \frac{x^3}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^1}{x^1+x^3}\\ 
&=&\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}
\end{eqnarray}

again $P(r_\mbox{obs}=1) + P(r_\mbox{obs}=2) + P(r_\mbox{obs}=3)=1$.


## Lastly: $r_\mbox{true}=3$ {-}

\begin{eqnarray}
P(r_\mbox{obs}=1)
&=& P({\mathbf 3}\succ 2\succ 1) + P({\mathbf 3}\succ 1\succ 2)\\
&=&\frac{x^3}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\\
&=&\frac{x^2}{1+x+x^2}\\
&{}&\\
P(r_\mbox{obs}=2) &=& P(1\succ {\mathbf 3}\succ 2) + P(2\succ {\mathbf
3}\succ 1)\\
&=& \frac{x^1}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^3}{x^2+x^3} +
    \frac{x^2}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^3}{x^1+x^3}\\
&=& \frac{x+2x^3+x^4}{(1+x)(1+x^2)(1+x+x^2)}
&{}&\\
P(r_\mbox{obs}=3) &=& P(1\succ 2\succ {\mathbf 3}) + P(2\succ 1\succ
{\mathbf 3})\\
&=& \frac{x^1}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^2}{x^1+x^3} +
    \frac{x^2}{x^1+x^2+x^3}\cdot\frac{x^1}{x^1+x^3}\\ 
&=& \frac{1+x+2x^2}{(1+x)(1+x^2)(1+x+x^2)}
\end{eqnarray}

again $P(r_\mbox{obs}=1) + P(r_\mbox{obs}=2) + P(r_\mbox{obs}=3)=1$
(although this is harder to see).

## inferences



$$
r_\mbox{true}=1\longrightarrow\begin{cases}
r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_1$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow\hat{r}=2\,\mbox{with probability $p_2$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow\hat{r}=3\,\mbox{with probability $p_3$}\\
\end{cases}
$$

$$
r_\mbox{true}=2\longrightarrow\begin{cases}
r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_1$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow\hat{r}=2\,\mbox{with probability $p_2$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow\hat{r}=3\,\mbox{with probability $p_3$}\\
\end{cases}
$$

$$
r_\mbox{true}=2\longrightarrow\begin{cases}
r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_1$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow\hat{r}=2\,\mbox{with probability $p_2$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow\hat{r}=3\,\mbox{with probability $p_3$}\\
\end{cases}
$$

### Bias for $r_\mbox{true}=1$:

\begin{eqnarray}
r_\mbox{true} &=& 1
\longrightarrow\\
\mathbb{E}(\hat{r}) &=& 
1\times\left(\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right) + 
2\times\left(\frac{1}{1+x}+\frac{1}{1+x^2}-\frac{2}{1+x+x^2}\right)  + 
3\times\left(1-\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{1+x^2}+\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right)\\
&=& 3-\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{1+x^2}\\
&\longrightarrow& 1\qquad\mbox{as $x\longrightarrow 0$}
\end{eqnarray}

### MSE for $r_\mbox{true}=1$:

\begin{eqnarray}
r_\mbox{true} &=& 1
\longrightarrow\\
\mathbb{E}(\hat{r}-r_\mbox{true})^2 &=& 
(1-1)^2\times\left(\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}   \right)  +
(2-1)^2\times\left(\frac{1}{1+x}+\frac{1}{1+x^2}-\frac{2}{1+x+x^2}\right)  + 
(3-1)^2\times\left(1-\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{1+x^2}+\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right)\\
&=&
\left(\frac{1}{1+x}+\frac{1}{1+x^2}-\frac{2}{1+x+x^2}\right)  + 
4\left(1-\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{1+x^2}+\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right)\\
&=& 4-\frac{3}{1+x}-\frac{3}{1+x^2}+\frac{2}{1+x+x^2}\\
&\longrightarrow& 0\qquad\mbox{as $x\longrightarrow 0$}
\end{eqnarray}

## Now $r_\mbox{true}=2$:

### Bias for $r_\mbox{true}=2$:

\begin{eqnarray}
r_\mbox{true} &=& 2
\longrightarrow\\
\mathbb{E}(\hat{r}) &=& 
1\times\left(\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}                        \right)  +
2\times\left(1-\frac{2x}{1+x+x^2}                     \right)  + 
3\times\left(\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}                        \right)\\ 
&=& 2 (!)
\end{eqnarray}

### MSE for $r_\mbox{true}=2$:

\begin{eqnarray}
r_\mbox{true} &=& 2
\longrightarrow\\
\mathbb{E}(\hat{r}-r_\mbox{true})^2 &=& 
(1-2)^2\times\left(\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}   \right)  +
(2-2)^2\times\left(1-\frac{2x}{1+x+x^2}\right)  + 
(3-2)^2\times\left(\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}\right)\\
&=&
\left(\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}\right)  + 
\left(\frac{x}{1+x+x^2}\right)\\
&=& \frac{2x}{1+x+x^2}\\
&\longrightarrow& 0\qquad\mbox{as $x\longrightarrow 0$}
\end{eqnarray}


## Now $r_\mbox{true}=3$:

### Bias for $r_\mbox{true}=3$:

\begin{eqnarray}
r_\mbox{true} &=& 3
\longrightarrow\\
\mathbb{E}(\hat{r}) &=& 
1\times\left(\frac{x^2}{1+x+x^2}                        \right)  +
2\times\left(\frac{x+2x^3+x^4}{(1+x)(1+x^2)(1+x+x^2)}   \right)  + 
3\times\left(\frac{1+x+2x^2  }{(1+x)(1+x^2)(1+x+x^2)}   \right)\\
&=& XXXX
\end{eqnarray}





Summarising, the **bias** $B$ is:

\begin{eqnarray}
r_\mbox{true} &=& 1\longrightarrow B=2-\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{1+x^2}\\
r_\mbox{true} &=& 2\longrightarrow B=0\\
r_\mbox{true} &=& 3\longrightarrow B= \frac{1}{1+x}+\frac{1}{1+x^2}-2
\end{eqnarray}

The mean square error
$\operatorname{MSE}(\hat{r})=\mathbb{E}(\hat{r}-r_\mbox{true})^2$ is


# Second case:  $x>\sqrt{(\sqrt{5}-1)/2}$

Here the lines are crossed [with $r_\mbox{obs}=2$] and so we have

$r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow \hat{r}=1$

$r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow \hat{r}=1$   sic!

$r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow \hat{r}=3$

$$
r_\mbox{true}=1\longrightarrow\begin{cases}
r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_1$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_2$}\qquad\mbox{sic}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow\hat{r}=3\,\mbox{with probability $p_3$}\\
\end{cases}
$$

$$
r_\mbox{true}=2\longrightarrow\begin{cases}
r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_1$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_2$}\qquad\mbox{sic}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow\hat{r}=3\,\mbox{with probability $p_3$}\\
\end{cases}
$$

$$
r_\mbox{true}=2\longrightarrow\begin{cases}
r_\mbox{obs}=1\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_1$}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=2\longrightarrow\hat{r}=1\,\mbox{with probability $p_2$}\qquad\mbox{sic}\\
r_\mbox{obs}=3\longrightarrow\hat{r}=3\,\mbox{with probability $p_3$}\\
\end{cases}
$$





## Summarising





## References {-}
